plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to reduce volatility.
Trim CRWD to lower concentration risk in single names.
Increase holdings in broad index ETF SPY for stability.
Boost allocation to income ETF QQQI for steady cash flow.
Initiate a small position in MSFU as a quality addition.
conservative.read: This plan prioritizes capital preservation, aiming for modest monthly gains with significantly reduced drawdown potential. Expect lower upside compared to other modes due to de-risking and cash buffering.

plan: Eliminate SOXL and SPAL to significantly cut leverage risk.
Trim SMH and CRWD to reduce concentration in semiconductors and AI/software.
Add XLK to increase exposure to the technology sector index.
Initiate a position in MRVL, a high potential risk/reward setup.
Maintain a moderate cash buffer for flexibility.
balanced.read: This balanced approach aims for steady growth while actively managing risk by reducing leverage and concentration. Expect moderate gains with controlled drawdowns, blending index exposure with select high-conviction names.

plan: Increase positions in leveraged ETFs SOXL and SPAL for amplified growth potential.
Boost concentration in SMH and CRWD, key tech/semiconductor areas.
Add MRVL and ALAB, two high risk/reward scanner setups.
Maintain a smaller cash buffer to maximize invested capital.
Utilize strict stop-loss discipline to manage amplified risk.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan seeks maximum growth by leaning into leverage and concentration, accepting significantly higher potential drawdowns and volatility. Daily rebalancing of leveraged ETFs can impact returns, and losses can be amplified rapidly.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) and high-beta single names (CRWD, SMH).
Significantly increase allocation to income ETF QQQI.
Add to broad index ETF SPY for stability and diversification.
Focus on holdings that generate regular monthly distributions.
Maintain a conservative stance to prioritize income over growth.
income.read: This income-focused plan prioritizes generating steady monthly distributions and minimizing volatility. While NAV can still fluctuate, the primary goal is reliable income, not capital appreciation, with very low expected drawdowns.
